data.title: Vertex Pharmaceuticals stock VRTX drops 1.24% after hours to $459.25. Q4 revenue: $3.19B (up 10% YoY).ALKYFTREK generates $380.1M. Net income: $1.2B.
data.description: Vertex Pharmaceuticals Inc. saw its shares fall by 1.24% in after-hours trading on Thursday night, reaching $459.25. This drop followed the company's report of fourth-quarter revenue totaling $3.19 billion, showing a 10% increase compared to the previous year. The stock had closed at $465.02 during regular trading hours, marking a 0.82% increase. The boost in revenue was mainly attributed to ALYFTREK, a cystic fibrosis treatment generating $380.1 million in the quarter. However, there was a decrease in revenue from TRIKAFTA, the company's therapy for cystic fibrosis patients aged 2 and above, which dropped to $2.57 billion from $2.72 billion year-over-year. Vertex Pharmaceuticals also disclosed a fourth-quarter 2025 net income of $1.2 billion.
The 10-year JGB yield eased earlier drops to remain unchanged at 2.23%.

data.title: PARKROYAL on Beach Road revamps Singapore MICE, prioritizing connections and experiences over meetings.
data.description: PARKROYAL on Beach Road in Singapore is changing the game for international events by prioritizing meaningful connections and unforgettable experiences over mundane meetings. This shift aims to provide global organizers with a fresh perspective on hosting events in Singapore, enhancing their overall experience. The initiative comes as a response to the evolving needs of the industry, where creating valuable connections and unique experiences are increasingly becoming crucial. With its revamped approach, PARKROYAL on Beach Road seeks to lead the way in transforming the landscape of international events, catering to the demands of a dynamic market.

data.title: Bitcoin open interest drops to levels not seen since 2024, now at $34B. Is Traditional Finance turning away from BTC on US macroeconomic concerns?
data.description: The open interest in Bitcoin has decreased to $34 billion as investor interest dwindles and traders pivot their concerns towards troubling US macroeconomic data. There are speculations about Traditional Finance (TradFi) withdrawing from Bitcoin.

BOJ'S TAMURA: Wage growth is projected to reach levels that align with the 2% inflation goal this year.
BOJ'S TAMURA: Japan's output gap is already positive, with limited supply capacity putting upward pressure on prices.
BOJ'S TAMURA: The BOJ is likely to continue adjusting interest rates in line with improvements in economic and price conditions.
BOJ'S TAMURA: Core inflation is gradually rising and is approaching a level that could be considered embedded.
BOJ'S TAMURA: There is a strong possibility that by this spring, inflation will have stabilized around 2%.
BOJ'S TAMURA: So far, past interest rate increases have had only a limited effect on Japan's economic activity.
BOJ'S TAMURA: Interest rates still have some way to go before reaching a level considered neutral for the economy.
BOJ'S TAMURA: Once the BOJ's policy rate goes above 1%, the stimulative impact of monetary policy is expected to gradually diminish.
BOJ'S TAMURA: Even with further rate hikes, overall monetary conditions are likely to stay accommodative.

data.title: Cineverse announces $3.0 million public offering of 1,500,000 shares of Class A common stock.
data.description: Cineverse Corp., a leading entertainment technology company and studio, revealed the pricing details of its public offering on February 12, 2026. The company, listed as CNVS on Nasdaq, disclosed the sale of 1,500,000 shares of its Class A common stock at a set price. This move signifies Cineverse's initiative to raise capital from the market.

data.title: Bitcoin's largest crash since 2021 leads to $2.3B loss, as short-term holders sell at steep declines in record event.
data.description: Bitcoin recently experienced its highest-ever realized losses during a significant capitulation event within the cryptocurrency market. Short-term holders were observed selling at substantial losses during Bitcoin’s decline.
